Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation when multiple bus objects with the same hierarchical structure are used
Description: If you use multiple bus objects with the same hierarchical structure but with different data types for some of the leaf elements, Real-Time Workshop generates incorrect typedef struct  definitions. In the generated code, Real-Time Workshop incorrectly defines such dissimilar bus objects to have the same struct  type.

Summary: Segmentation fault when generating code for a model using variant subsystems
Description: You might see a segmentation fault when you generate code for a model containing variant subsystems, if You are generating preprocessor conditionals for those variant subsystems Any of the variants contain a subsystem that generates a reusable function, and that subsystem is reused elsewhere in the model, outside of any variant subsystem.  ?
Workaround: To avoid this problem, for all subsystems inside variant subsystems, on the Code Generation tab of the Block Parameter dialog, set the value of Function packaging to Function or Inline, so that the code generator does not attempt to generate reusable functions for these subsystems.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Code Generation for initialization of states that have fixed-point or enumerated data types.
Description: The generated code for initializing a block state might be incorrect if any of the following are true: The state data type is a fixed-point data type with a nonzero bias, and the initial value of the state is specified as the value of the bias.  The state data type is an enumerated data type whose default enumeration does not map to the integer value zero, and the initial value of the state is specified as the enumeration that maps to zero.  The state data type is a structure that has an element that falls into one of the previous two cases, or the structure has a substructure with such an element.  In the case of the fixed-point data type, the state might be initialized to the real world value zero. In the case of the enumerated data type, the state might be initialized to the default enumeration.

Summary: Simulink Verification and Validation tool may throw errors or warnings while generating reports
Description: When generating reports through Simulink Verification and Validation tool, errors or warnings similar to the following may appear and report creation may fail: In component rptgen.cml_eval : No appropriate method, property, or field isInsertString for class rptgen.cml_eval.
Workaround: ?Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Calls to custom-code functions from Stateflow transitions are sometimes incorrectly optimized away
Description: One of the code generation optimizations tries to identify identical code in both branches of an if-else construct and replace them with a single, unconditional copy of the branch code. For example, the optimization converts the pattern labeled "BEFORE" to the pattern labeled "AFTER": BEFORE: if(c==1) {   x = x+1;   y = sin(1);}else{   x = x+1;   y = sin(1);} AFTER:    x = x+1;   y = sin(1); This optimization sometimes identifies calls to different custom-code functions as being the same and wrongly optimizes them. Specifically, under the following conditions, calls to custom-code functions from Stateflow transitions get incorrectly optimized away: 1. The diagram has a flow-graph construct that results in the following pattern: if(condition) {   foo();}else{   bar();} where foo() and bar() are user functions defined in custom C-code. Note that the calling signatures for foo() and bar() must be identical if they have any input arguments. For example, if foo() and bar() have an input argument, then the calls should be passing in identical inputs as in {foo(2), bar(2)} or {foo(a+b), bar(a+b)}. 2. The option for Simulink block / Stateflow object comments  is turned off in the Configuration Parameters dialog. Existence of traceability comments or non-identical user-comments causes the pattern match to fail and the bug does not occur. Under these two conditions, the optimization incorrectly infers that foo() and bar() are identical and replaces the above construct with just: foo();
Workaround: In the Comments pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog, select Simulink block / Stateflow object comments. This option creates distinct comments in the if-else branches that stop this optimization from doing this incorrect pattern match.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier sporadically throws an exception during the compatibility check.
Description: Simulink Design Verifier sporadically throws an exception during compatibility checking of a model that contains state variables that are read multiple times, for example, Data Store Memory blocks.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: For charts with local events, exit actions sometimes get removed from generated code
Description: Suppose that a Stateflow chart satisfies all of the following conditions: It has local events, either explicit or implicit. It has a parent state with two or more substates. Some (but not all) child substates have exit actions.  Under certain circumstances, some of the exit actions can disappear from the generated code. The attached model mg702315.mdl demonstrates the problem. In this model, when the outer state "A" is exited, the exit action of "A2" needs to execute. However, when debugging is turned off, or when code is generated for the model, the exit action is not executed. The attached function findProblemStates  finds any states that are affected by this issue. Please see its help for how to use it. Running this function as: >> findProblemStates('mg702315'); after opening the attached model will print out the following message at the MATLAB prompt: Warning: State A has some child states whose exit actions might disappear! with the state name (in this case "A") hyperlinked to open the state.
Workaround: This bug can be avoided by executing the following command once per sesson before generating code:sf('feature','Optimized Exit Fcns',0);You can add this command to your startup.m file so that it executes in every session. You can also add this command to the file toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/private/sfinit.m so that it executes every time you load a chart. Doing so helps you avoid this bug in the generated code.The only other effect of this command is to turn off a minor readability feature that minimizes switch statements in state exit functions. No observable effect on the code efficiency exists when you turn off this optimization.

Summary: Model Explorer search can cause error if you search again or open the Contents pane
Description: After you perform a search using the Model Explorer and then you either select the Contents  tab or you perform another search, you may see an error that starts with text similar to the following: ??? No appropriate method, property, or field Name for class DAStudio.MEViewProperty. Error in ==> DAStudio.MEViewManager.enableLiveliness>clearTransientProperties at 259                 if strcmp(view.Properties(index).Name, view.GroupName) Error in ==> DAStudio.MEViewManager.enableLiveliness>doSearchPropertiesAdded at 215         clearTransientProperties(actView); Error in ==> DAStudio.MEViewManager.enableLiveliness>syncMEViewManagerFromME at 89     doSearchPropertiesAdded(eventData, manager); Warning: Error occurred while evaluating listener callback.
Workaround: To recover from this error:Near the top of the Model Explorer Contents pane, select Show Details (if the column view details section does not already appear).In the column view details section, click the Options button.Select Reset This View to Factory Settings.

Summary: Incorrect explicit type cast to custom data type in generated code when Prelookup block uses Simulink.NumericType
Description: A custom data type is used in lookup table utility functions. Currently, Simulink.NumericType  or Simulink.AliasType  type names cannot be used in the shared utility code that is generated from lookup table blocks.

Summary: Model block SIL/PIL error when using Function Prototype Control and tunable parameters
Description: The following Model block SIL/PIL error occurs when the component under test uses Function Prototype Control to specify a step function return value (for an outport) and  has tunable parameters: Error using ==> CodeInfoUtils.CodeInfoUtils>CodeInfoUtils.getDataInterfacesForFunction at 525 CAT arguments dimensions are not consistent.

Summary: Sample time propagation issue in Simulink can adversely impact some Simscape models
Description: Sample time propagation issue in Simulink can adversely impact some Simscape models. This Simulink issue causes a port-based sample time block to have a continuous, [0,0], sample time on an input port. Instead, this block should have a fixed in minor step, [0,1], sample time. If the input signal to a Simscape S-PS Converter block has a fixed in minor step sample time (for example, Rate Transition block output), Simscape might fail to react to discontinuities in the input signal. For Simulink, a port-based sample time block (for example, a Simscape block) must have the following conditions for the issue to occur: Inherits a fixed in minor step sample time on an input port Inherits a continuous sample time on all output ports Has a subsystem boundary between the input port with the inherited fixed in minor step sample time and the block that propagates [0,1] to it Exists in a model for which inline parameters are disabled.  This issue originated in Simulink in R12.1, but did not impact Simscape until R2010b.

Summary: Segmentation violation when generating code for all variant subsystems if Data Store Memory blocks are missing
Description: A segmentation violation occurs when generating code for all variants if Data Store Read blocks in variant subsystems do not have corresponding Data Store Memory blocks. You should see an error instead.
Workaround: Ensure that a corresponding Data Store Memory block exists for every Data Store Read blocks used in variant subsystems.

Summary: Junctions disappear inside subcharts that are grouped
Description: A data-structure corruption occurs when a subchart with junctions is grouped (for example, by double-clicking it) and then a hierarchy rebuild occurs (for example, when you navigate up out of the subchart and then back into it). After the hierarchy rebuild, the junctions in the subchart might still be visible, but the firstJunction  property is lost.  As a result, the junctions eventually become invisible (for example, if you save/load the model or navigate up and down the hierarchy twice).
Workaround: Do not group a subchart. If your model is already corrupted or you suspect it is corrupted, extract attached zip and run the corruptionDetector.m script.  This script detects corruptions in your model and explains how to fix it manually. Only one edit to the model file is required per corrupted subchart.

Summary: Segmentation violation when displaying variant subsystem if the mask drawing code contains set_params
Description: If the mask icon drawing code of blocks inside inactive variants contains a set_param call, then you may see a segmentation violation when Simulink tries to render the block on the screen.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, do not change a value on the block during drawing. Do not use set_param in the mask icon display section.

Summary: When multiple constraints are associated to a single model object, Simulink Design Verifier throws an error when generating the analysis report.
Description: When multiple constraints, such as sldv.condition , are specified on the same model object, Simulink Design Verifier cannot generate the analysis report.

Summary: Identical For Each subsystems may cause TLC code generation error
Description: A TLC error may occur when you generate code for a model if the model contains two idential subsystems that each contains both : A For Each block A subsystem that also contains a For Each block

Summary: Variant subsystem with preprocessor conditionals doesn't work in simplified initialization mode.
Description: Enabling 'Generate preprocessor conditionals' on a Variant subsystem will not work with 'Underspecified initialization detection' is set to Simplified.

Summary: Assertion during code generation for Switch block
Description: Code generation might cause an assertion under all of the following conditions: A [m,n]  matrix signal is the input to a Selector block, which produces a [1,n]  output signal. The Selector block output signal connects to a Switch block, which has at least one other [n]  input signal.
Workaround: Reshape the [n] input signal(s) connected to the Switch block to [1,n].

Summary: Using matrices of structures in Embedded MATLAB code might produce incorrect results
Description: You might get incorrect simulation results or generated code if you use matrices in Embedded MATLAB code where each element is a structure that contains at least one matrix field. This bug can occur in Embedded MATLAB Function blocks in Simulink models, Embedded MATLAB functions in Stateflow charts, and code compiled with emlc . For an example, see the attached MATLAB file CompositeMatrices.m . To reproduce the problem, follow these steps: Produce baseline results by running CompositeMatrices  in MATLAB Build a MEX file using this command: emlc CompositeMatrices  Run the MEX file CompositeMatrices . The MEX results differ from the MATLAB results.

Summary: Incorrect block initialization and code generation when a subsystem is inside another subsystem that contains a For Each block
Description: Generated code does not correctly initialize the block output, dwork or zero-crossing for a block inside a function-call subsystem under the following conditions: The block is not inside a subsystem in that function-call subsystem The function-call subsystem is inside another subsystem which contains a For Each block
Workaround: As a workaround, add an atomic subsystem in such function-call subsystem and move the block into this atomic subsystem.

Summary: MATLAB ceases execution without any stack trace or error while simulating with implicit Simulink solver
Description: An uncaught memmory allocation error causes MATLAB to stop running without any stack trace or error. This memory allocation exception occours when the model has large dimension signals and uses:  An implicit solver  A solver Jacobian method other than full perturbation  This result is due to the need for more memory and time to get the sparse pattern information.
Workaround: On the Solver pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, select 'Full perturbation' for the solver Jaobian method parameter.

Summary: Bus Assignment, Bus Creator, and Bus Selector block dialogs display an error when any of these blocks are used in a Simulink function inside a Stateflow chart
Description: When a Bus Assignment, Bus Creator, or Bus Selector block is used in a Simulink function inside a Stateflow chart, and that block is double-clicked, the block dialog does not open correctly. Instead, the following error appears: Error occurred when trying to create dialogNo appropriate method, property, or field Parent for class Stateflow.Chart. ? ?
Workaround: Installation instructions       Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.       Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.       Exit MATLAB.       Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.                     Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file:                                    Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.                     Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>                                          Extract the files from the .zip file.                                                               Windows Systems:                           Open the .zip file.                                  Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.                                                                                                Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):                            cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>                                                 Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Change log:February 2, 2011: attachment_694778_10b_2011-02-02.zip :inital version, works on R2010b only.

Summary: Generated code may fail to compile for variant subsystems that are controlled by C preprocessor conditionals
Description: The code generated for the C API may fail to compile when the model contains variant subsystems controlled by C preprocessor conditionals. The problem is caused by the code referencing the address of parameter data that is excluded from compilation due to the preprocessor conditionals.

Summary: Compiler error or warning when code generated for variant subsystems controlled by C preprocessor conditionals includes tunable parameters
Description: The code generated for the C API may contain an empty data structure for the model's tunable parameters when the model contains variant subsystems controlled by C preprocessor conditionals.  This empty structure may be treated as an error by the compiler and can result in a warning or error.

Summary: Parameter mismatch error during code generation when mixing R2010b models with models created in earlier releases
Description: If you generate code for a model in which models created in R2010b are mixed with models from earlier releases, an error similar to the following might occur: The following Configuration parameter option(s) do not match between the models 'x' and 'y':Hardware Implementation- ProdLargestAtomicInteger (Long != Char)ProdLargestAtomicInteger is mapped to 'integer:'- ProdLargestAtomicFloat (Double != None)ProdLargestAtomicFloat is mapped to 'floating-point:' This error is likely to occur if both of the following conditions exist: At least one R1010b model and at least one model from an earlier release coexist in a model reference hierarchy or share a utilities directory.  The models have their hardware configured to a device that does not match at least one of the following settings: Number of bits: int  set to 32 Largest atomic size: integer  set to Char Largest atomic size: floating point  set to None   (For example, this condition could apply to Freescale 32-bit PowerPC, which does not match the second and third settings.) Example devices include: Most Freescale devices Analog Devices: Blackfin, SHARC, or TigerSHARC ARM Compatible: ARM 7, ARM 8, ARM 9, ARM 10, ARM 11, or ARM Cortex Texas Instruments: C6000 or C2000 All 16-bit integer devices
Workaround: To work around this bug in R2010b, for each model from an earlier release, go to the Hardware Implementation pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box and toggle the hardware device selection. This reapplies the hardware device characteristics to the model, making the values consistent with the R2010b model.

Summary: Requesting the edit-time signal hierarchy crashes MATLAB if a block with a broken link exists upstream
Description: When you request the edit-time signal hierarchy for a signal, if a broken link block exists upstream on the path of a signal, then MATLAB crashes instead of producing an error message. You can request edit-time signal hierarchy either by opening the Bus Creator, Bus Selector, or Bus Assignment block dialog, or by calling the get_param  method on a port handle, using the SignalHierarchy  property.
Workaround: Update your model. During the update process, Simulink reports broken links as an error.Resolve the broken link.Request the edit-time signal hierarchy.

Summary: Code Reuse Subsystem between two nested For Each Subsystems might cause segment violation when generating code for a Simulink model?
Description: A segment violation might occur when generating code for a Simulink model under the following conditions: 1. One instance of a Code Reuse Subsystem contains an inner For Each Subsystem, and is inside another For Each Subsystem. 2. The type of the Code Reuse Subsystem is not For Each Subsystem. 3. The code generation option of the inner For Each Subsystem is "Reusable function" or "Auto". 4. There is another instance of the same Code Reuse Subsystem that is not inside any For Each Subsystem. ?
Workaround: Manually insert a Signal Conversion Block in front of the inner For Each Subsystem contained by the Code Reuse Subsystem.

Summary: Model with array of buses signal may not generate accurate code
Description: Code generation for may be inaccurate when all  of the following conditions are true: The model contains an array of buses signal. The array of buses signal has the Struct  custom storage class specified (via Signal Properties dialog box or a Simulink.Signal  object). The Configuration Parameters -> Code Generation -> SIL and PIL Verification -> Create Block  option is set to SIL .  When code is generated for this kind of model, the compilation process may error. The output of the compilation process looks similar to the following: modelname_sf.c(344) : error C2088: '[' : illegal for struct modelname_sf.c(345) :error C2088: '[' : illegal for struct modelname_sf.c(352) :error C2088: '[' : illegal for struct modelname_sf.c(353) : error C2088: '[' : illegal for struct

Summary: Generated code may fail to compile for reused variant subsystems that are controlled by C preprocessor conditionals
Description: The generated code may fail to compile when the model contains variant subsystems that are reused as well as controlled by C preprocessor conditionals. The problem is caused by the code referencing the address of non-constant parameter data that is excluded from compilation due to the preprocessor conditionals.

Summary: Code generation errors with arrays of buses and custom storage classes
Description: Indexing code for arrays of buses signal lines in a Simulink model may be incorrect when generating code from the model. The incorrect code will appear in the output and/or the update methods if both  of the following conditions are true: The model has at least one array of buses signal. The custom GetSet  storage class is specified for the array of buses signal (via the Signal Properties dialog box or a Simulink.Signal  object).  Under these conditions, if TLC assertions have been turned on (using the Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Debugging  option), then a TLC assertion similar to the following may be thrown: Error: File: (matlabroot)/toolbox/simulink/simulink/@Simulink/tlc/GetSet.tlc Line: 56 Column: 9 Assertion failed
Workaround: See EBR 923193.

Summary: Parameters UnderspecifiedInitializationDetection and MergeDetectMultiDrivingBlocksExec are not saved in MATLAB file when using the saveAs method of the Simulink.ConfigSet object
Description: When using the saveAs method of the Simulink.ConfigSet object, the parameters UnderspecifiedInitializationDetection  and MergeDetectMultiDrivingBlocksExec  are not saved in the resulting MATLAB file. Consequently, the Simulink.ConfigSet object loaded from the MATLAB file will always have the default values for these parameters. The default values UnderspecifiedInitializationDetection  and MergeDetectMultiDrivingBlocksExec  are 'Simplified' and 'none', respectively. As an example, the problem is illustrated for the parameter UnderspecifiedInitializationDetection  by the following MATLAB command sequence: >> currConfigset = Simulink.ConfigSetcurrConfigset =Simulink.ConfigSet (handle)  Description: ''  Components: [8x1 Simulink.ConfigComponent]  Name: 'Configuration'  >> get_param(currConfigset, 'UnderspecifiedInitializationDetection')  ans =  Classic  >> set_param(currConfigset, 'UnderspecifiedInitializationDetection', 'Simplified')>> get_param(currConfigset, 'UnderspecifiedInitializationDetection')  ans =  Simplified  >> currConfigset.saveAs('modifiedConfigset.m')>> clear currConfigset>> currConfigset = modifiedConfigsetcurrConfigset =Simulink.ConfigSet (handle)  Description: ''  Components: [8x1 Simulink.ConfigComponent]  Name: 'Configuration'  >> get_param(currConfigset, 'UnderspecifiedInitializationDetection')  ans =  Classic  >>

Summary: Code for variant subsystems may define unnecessary data
Description: The code generated for a model containing variant subsystems controlled by C preprocessor conditionals may fail to conditionally compile those subsystems' constant and unstructured parameter data. As a consequence, the compiled executable is not optimally efficient because it defines data that is never used.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model reference with variable-size signal with non-Auto storage class
Description: Simulink might give an incorrect answer and Real-Time Workshop might generate incorrect code for a model, which contains a model block and its referenced model has either of the following: A variable-size root inport and the output signal of the root Inport block has an ExportedGlobal , ImportedExtern,  or ImportedExternalPointer  storage class. A variable-size root outport and the input signal of the root Outport block has an ExportedGlobal , ImportedExtern,  or ImportedExternalPointer  storage class.

Summary: In Japanese Windows 7 environments, MATLAB Report Generator generates unreadable text
Description: In a Japanese Windows 7 environment, for PDF documents, MATLAB Report Generator and Simulink Report Generator may generate garbled or incorrect text.
Workaround: ?Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file for your release (for example, attachment_706307_9b_2011-02-07.zip for R2009b) and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Subsystem to referenced model conversion may cause a segmentation violation
Description: If a subsystem has one or more Subsystem Variants blocks as children, then Simulink may crash with a segmentation violation if you convert that subsystem to a referenced model.

Summary: When a model that contains Simscape blocks is simulated in Accelerator mode, a segmentation violation might occur
Description: When a model that contains Simscape blocks is simulated in Accelerator mode, a segmentation violation might occur. You might see this behavior if you rerun the model after you edit the Simscape blocks.
Workaround: As a workaround, in the file matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/accelreg.tlc replace line%<FcnGenInitMassMatrixPrInRoot()>withif (ssGetSizeofDWork(S) == sizeof(%<::tDWorkType>)) {%<FcnGenInitMassMatrixPrInRoot()>}

Summary: A warning for a minimum step size violation causes Simulink to become unresponsive
Description: In very rare situations, the warning for a minimum step size violation is thrown consecutively and Simulink becomes unresponsive. You cannot stop the simulation without terminating MATLAB. This bug is fixed and you can use CTRL-C in the MATLAB command window to stop the simulation.
Workaround: By adjusting solver parameters, such as Relative tolerance, Absolute tolerance, or Initial step size, you can avoid the warning for a minimum step size violation.

Summary: Underspecified initialization detection parameter value incorrectly displayed on Japanese machine
Description: On a Japanese machine, on the Diagnostics > Data Validity  pane of the Configuration Parameter dialog box, the Underspecified initialization detection  parameter always displays Classic , even when the parameter is set to Simplified .

Summary: Non-Compilable code for terminate function of ForEach Subsystem
Description: The code generator might generate a non-compilable terminate function for a ForEach subsystem. This might occur when you right-click a virtual subsystem, select C/C++ Code , and then Export Functions .
Workaround: Do not generate a terminate function. On the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation > Interface pane, clear the Terminate function required parameter.

Summary: If a referenced model's Model arguments have changed, opening a parent model with Port and parameter mismatch set to error or warning may crash Simulink
Description: Simulink crashes when all  of the following are true: You are opening a model where Diagnostics > Model Referencing > Port and parameter mismatch  is set to error  or warning . A mismatch is reported and is caused by a change to a referenced model's Model arguments. One of the mismatched Model arguments is the empty string.
Workaround: Change the referenced model so that the Model arguments mismatch error does not involve the empty string. If the crash is occurring because the referenced model was changed:From not having Model arguments to having Model arguments, then open the referenced model, remove the Model arguments, and save the referenced model.From having Model arguments to not having Model arguments, then open the referenced model, add one or more Model arguments, and save the referenced model.On the parent model, set Diagnostics > Model Referencing > Port and parameter mismatch to none.In the referenced model, set the Model arguments to the desired value.In the parent model, refresh the Model blocks by selecting Edit > Links and Model Blocks > Refresh.On the parent model, set Diagnostics > Model Referencing > Port and parameter mismatch to its previous value.The parent model should no longer crash when you open it.

Summary: MATLAB hangs while simulating a model using an implicit solver
Description: For a model with large dimension signals, using an implicit solver in combination with any solver Jacobian method other than full perturbation may cause MATLAB to hang. This result is due to the large memory and time required to get the sparse pattern information. ? ?
Workaround: On the Solver pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, select 'Full perturbation' for the solver Jaobian method parameter.

Summary: Software-in-the-loop (SIL) simulation may fail when code coverage is enabled
Description: If you run a SIL simulation and use the BullseyeCoverage tool to collect coverage metrics, you may get an error that includes the following message: An error occurred while calling into the SIL or PIL target connectivity implementation. There must be exactly one probe with empty column number This error occurs when the generated code uses a ternary operator and  the code is formatted such that BullseyeCoverage inserts a probe to measure both a condition and a decision at the first non-whitespace character on a line. For example, the following code will trigger this error:       14 xcv3 = =>F  15 (xcv1==0) && =>t       ^ =>t  16 (xcv2==1)  =>f  17 && (xcv2==0) ?      18 1 : 0;

Summary: Incorrect "class mismatch" errors for local scope data of Truth Table block
Description: If local scope data is defined and used in a Truth Table block, a model might not simulate due to incorrect "class mismatch" errors. For example, you might see an error that looks something like this: Class mismatch (double ~= single).The class to the left is the class of the left-hand side of the assignment.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier ignores specified input minimum and maximum values when Test Condition or Proof Assumption block is also specified on the same signal.
Description: Simulink Design Verifier ignores specified input minimum and maximum values in certain cases. This bug surfaces in some cases when a Test Condition or Proof Assumption block is also specified on the connecting signal and when the model contains certain math operation blocks, such as sqrt, etc.

Summary: In Bus Assignment and Bus Selector blocks, unexpected selection after repeatedly using the Remove button
Description: When you use the Remove  button to remove a signal from the selected list for a Bus Assignment or Bus Selector block, Simulink removes the selected signal and then automatically selects the next signal and  the first signal in the selection list. If you press the Remove  button again, Simulink removes the two signals that it automatically selected.
Workaround: Do not press the Remove button twice in a row. Instead:Select the first signal to remove.Press the Remove button.Manually select the next signal that you want to remove.Press the Remove button.

Summary: Code generator might produce incorrect code for referenced model containing reusable system?
Description: Incorrect code might occur under all of the following conditions: Referenced model has a vector or matrix input. The referenced model immediately selects the first element of this input signal, by using a Selector or Demux block. The selected signal is routed to an inner subsystem with no intervening operations.  The generated code might be missing a subscript for the selected model reference argument. To see the error, build the attached model and open childModel.c . You can see the error in the first call to childModel_pickA : Incorrect: childModel_pickA(&rtu_dataArray, &(*rty_out)); Correct: childModel_pickA(&rtu_dataArray[1], &(*rty_out)); When the target language is C++, some compilers might error during compilation with an error message, which explains the incompatibility between the actual and formal arguments. For example, the Microsoft C++ compiler might produce the following error message: cannot convert parameter 2 from 'const real32_T *[]' to 'const real32_T *'Types pointed to are unrelated; conversion requires reinterpret_cast, C-style cast or function-style cast
Workaround: As a workaround, you can do one of the following:Specify the inner reusable subsystem as Inlined.Add a non-virtual block before or after the Selector block.

Summary: MATLAB stops responding to user input after you copy text, and then open a Simulink UI such as Library Browser, Model Explorer, or some block dialogs
Description: After copying text from a Simulink element (such as a block, signal label, or annotation), opening some UI windows or dialogs will cause MATLAB to stop responding. This occurs on all Windows platforms.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Bitwise OR and bitwise XOR return wrong values for Fixed Point inputs with non-zero bias
Description: This bug might occur when a bitwise OR or bitwise XOR is applied to a fixed point value with a non-zero bias. A code generation optimization which simplifies 0 | X  to X  is incorrectly applied when the real-world value of the constant is zero, instead of when the stored integer value is zero. For example, if the type of the constant is sfix19_Sp3_Bp3  (scaling is 0.3 and bias is 0.3) and the stored integer is -1, then the real-world value is 0, which causes the code generator to incorrectly apply the optimization.
Workaround: Use a Data Type Conversion block to convert the operands of the bitwise operator to simple integer types.

Summary: sldvmakeharness and slvnvmakeharness do not update correctly for models that are configured to load input data from a workspace before the simulation begins.
Description: If a model is configured to load input data from a workspace before the simulation begins, and you run the sldvmakeharness  or slvnvmakeharness  function to generate a harness model for that model, the generated harness model doesn't update successfully.
Workaround: Before you run sldvmakeharness or slvnvmakeharness to generate a harness model, make sure to disable loading input data from a workspace before the simulation begins.

Summary: Bus-capable block that uses a MATLAB structure for Initial Value or Initial Condition parameters and that is in a referenced model in Accelerator mode can cause a build error
Description: Updating a top-level model can result in a build error when all  of these conditions apply: The top-level model references a model that is in Accelerator mode. The referenced model contains a bus-capable block (such as a Memory or Merge block) that uses a MATLAB structure for its Initial Value  or Initial Condition  parameter.  ?
Workaround: If the specified MATLAB structure does not have the exact same structure and attributes as that of the incoming bus (that is, the structure is a partial structure), consider using the Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct command to create a matching full structure. Then use that generated full structure for the bus-capable block parameter.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generation of literal constant as unsigned
Description: Real-Time Workshop software might generate an unsigned constant in a expression, that is expected to be evaluated using a signed type. The unsigned constant forces the expression to evaluate using an unsigned data type, which causes a simulation and code generation behavior mismatch. You can detect this failure condition using Polyspace or any MISRA compliance checker.
Workaround: You can use the following command to disable unsigned constants emission for constants of type unsigned char and unsigned short:>> slfeature('CGForceUnsignedConsts', 0)

Summary: Edit-Time requests for signal hierarchy information followed by a model update may lead to a segmentation violation.
Description: At edit-time, users can request signal hierarchy information by executing any one of the following tasks: Open dialog for Bus Creator/Selector/Assignment blocks Executing get_param(<portHandle>, 'SignalHierarchy')   If any one of these actions precedes a model update, then Simulink might trigger a segmentation violation.
Workaround: There are two potential work arounds:Update the model before performing any of the edit-time tasks.Copy the attached file, attachment_684927_20110412.zip, in a temp dir and unzip it.Execute attachment_684927_10b.p before opening your model.Note that you can place a call to this P-file in your startup.m file, however; this may lead to longer MATLAB initialization times since this call will load Simulink as well.

Summary: Saturation block does not saturate remaining fixed-point matrix elements when one input is within threshold
Description: When the following conditions are all true, a Saturation block does not saturate some input elements during simulation: The data type of the input or output is of fixed-point type. The input is a matrix. One element of the matrix input is within the lower and upper limit of the Saturation block.  The input elements that follow the in-threshold element are not saturated.

Summary: Indexing into scalar with variable-size indices causes assertion failure during code generation.
Description: The problem occurs when this code pattern is used: x(I,J) = s; under the following conditions: x  and s  must be scalars (size 1x1). One or both of I  and J  must be variable sized. There must be at least two subscripts. The problem does not occur for assignments of the form x(I) = s .  The following internal error message is generated during code generation: Assertion failed numIndices == aMatrixType->numDimensions() at line 782 of file "dom_core/utility/cg_sanity_checker.cpp".
Workaround: Rewrite the assignment as a loop:for i = 1:numel(I)  for j = 1:numel(J)    x(I(i),J(j)) = s;  endend

Summary: Model with machine-parented data and only linked Stateflow charts produces TLC error during code generation
Description: Suppose that a model has machine-parented data and contains only linked Stateflow charts that belong to other library models (that is, the model does not contain any non-linked Stateflow charts). Generating code for the main model results in a TLC error with the following symptom: Error: File: ...\matlab\stateflow\c\tlc\sflib.tlc Line: 403 Column: 29Unable to find StateflowMachineVarGroupVarIdx within the VarGroups scope
Workaround: Delete the machine-parented data in the main model. Because the main model does not have any non-linked Stateflow charts, this data object is redundant. Note that machine-parented data from a main model cannot be accessed by linked charts.

Summary: Code generation results differ from simulation results when Probe block input is a variable-size signal with size or dimensions greater than range of selected output data type
Description: When a variable-size signal is input to a Probe block, there can be a difference between simulation and code-generation results for the "Probe width" and "Probe signal dimensions" outputs. Such a difference is due to an input width or input signal dimension being large enough to cause an overflow. In particular, this bug occurs when both the following conditions are true:  The input to a Probe block is a variable-size signal. The input signal width cannot be represented within the range of the data type selected for the width output, or the input signal dimension cannot be represented within the range of the data type selected for the signal dimension output.
Workaround: This bug occurs due to a difference in overflow and rounding modes between simulation and generated code for the Probe block. It manifests itself  for variable-size signal inputs. To detect whether this bug occurs in your model, extract and run the attached detection script as follows:detectProbeOverflowRoundingBug('modelname') The script returns 'true' if the bug manifests itself in your model. To work around this issue, change the selected data type for width and signal dimensions to 'double' and insert 'Data Type Conversion' blocks at the output with the following parameters (use default values for unspecified parameters): Output data type: [Your desired type]Integer rounding mode: NearestSaturate on integer overflow: on

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Merge block with constant input might generate incorrect code
Description: The code generator might produce incorrect code for a Merge block if all of the following conditions exist: Merge block has one or more constant inputs and at least one non-constant input One of the constant inputs is inside an atomic subsystem Atomic subsystem has a constant sample time  To identify signals and subsystems that have a constant sample time, in the model window, select Format > Sample Time Display  > Constant . This setting causes constant signals to appear magenta.
Workaround: Change the sample time of the atomic subsystem to match the output sample time of the Merge block.

Summary: Code generation can fail when Table Lookup block refers to tunable structure parameter
Description: When a Table Lookup block refers to an element of a tunable parameter structure, code generation can fail due to an assertion similar to the following: Code generation assertion 'var->type()->scope() == fScope || var->type()->equals(fVar2Type[var])' failed in 'rtwcg_utils/RTWCGMoveGlobals.cpp:306'[  0] 0x00007ff789dd0ad9 /matlabroot/matlab/bin/glnxa64/../../bin/glnxa64/libmwfl.so+00539353 fl::sysdep::linux::unwind_stack(void const**, unsigned long, unsigned long)+000009[  1] 0x00007ff789d7961b /matlabroot/matlab/bin/glnxa64/../../bin/glnxa64/libmwfl.so+00181787 fl::diag::stacktrace_base::capture(unsigned long)+000027[  2] 0x00007ff74b184fe8 /matlabroot/matlab/bin/glnxa64/../../bin/glnxa64/libmwrtwcg.so+00786408 rtwcg_assertion_failed(char const*, int, char const*)+000120[  3] 0x00007ff74b151923 /matlabroot/matlab/bin/glnxa64/../../bin/glnxa64/libmwrtwcg.so+00575779[  4] 0x00007ff74b23d76a /matlabroot/matlab/bin/glnxa64/../../bin/glnxa64/libmwrtwcg.so+01541994 RTWCG::RTWFcnConst::CommonTransformsToOptimizeAndEmitCode(RTWCG::TransformDispatcher&, PtrSet*, char const*)+008698 Alternatively, a Target Language Compiler (TLC) error, similar to the following, might result: Error: File: /matlabroot/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/lib/sysarglib.tlc Line: 16 Column: 45Unable to find CanonicalPrmArgDef within the Interface scopeMain program:==> [00] /matlabroot/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/lib/sysarglib.tlc:LibAccessCanPrmArg(16)    [01] /matlabroot/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/lib/paramlib.tlc:FcnGetCanParmArg(127)    [02] /matlabroot/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/lib/paramlib.tlc:FcnBlockParameterAddr(1097)    [03] /matlabroot/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/lib/paramlib.tlc:FcnBlockParameterBaseAddr(1231)    [04] /matlabroot/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/lib/paramlib.tlc:LibBlockParameterBaseAddr(1255)

Summary: n-D Lookup Table block might return different answers in simulation and code generation when input data type and corresponding breakpoint data type have different scaling
Description: When the input data type has different scaling from the corresponding breakpoint data type for an n-D Lookup table block, code generation results might differ from simulation results.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, set the breakpoint data type to Inherit: Same as corresponding input on the Data Types tab for the n-D Lookup Table block.

Summary: On Windows the Simulink Editor may become unresponsive when trying to drag a signal line in a locked library
Description: If you attempt to drag the signal arrow head in a locked library, a dialog pops up asking to "unlock" or "cancel." The dialog and the Simulink Editor may then become unresponsive.
Workaround: A binary fix is required. Here are the installation instructions:Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Extract the files from the .zip file.Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Accelerator mode simulation fails with a link error for a model containing an S-Function block for which additional S-function modules are specified (LCC compiler only)
Description: Accelerator mode simulation of a model fails with a link error if all of the following conditions exist: The model contains an S-Function block. Additional S-function modules are specified using the S-Function block parameter S-function modules  (SFunctionModules ). You are using the LCC compiler for model builds.  The link error occurs after you initiate the model simulation, during the build of the Accelerator mode simulation target.
Workaround: To work around this bug, download the attached .zip file and extract its contents to your MATLAB installation tree, using the following steps:On your system, log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation root folder by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the .zip file attached to this Bug Report and place the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Examine the contents of the .zip file and, for each file, rename or make a backup copy of the equivalent file in your MATLAB installation tree. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/attachment_679394_10b_win32_2010-11-02.zipExtract the files from the .zip file. Windows systems:Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the .zip file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):cd <MATLAB>unzip attachment_679394_10b_win32_2010-11-02.zipDelete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Generated software-in-the-loop block may have incorrect code
Description: You might get incorrect code when generating an software-in-the-loop block for a subsystem, if all of the following are true: The subsystem input or output has fixed-point data with a length larger than 64-bit The fixed-point data is a complex scalar  ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: possible incorrect results when a signal with an atomic data type propagates through an auto-inserted or virtual-bus driven Rate Transition block
Description: Beginning in Release 2010b, code generation for a Rate Transition block optimizes away unnecessary semaphore protection or double-buffering if the data type of the input signal is atomic. A data type is atomic  if it can be atomically loaded and stored on the target hardware, that is, if the size of the data type is less than or equal to the Largest atomic type  value displayed on the Hardware Implementation  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. However, in two scenarios, this optimization might generate code in which a local variable is used before it is initialized. Executing the code might produce incorrect numerical results. The following two scenarios might produce incorrect generated code. Scenario 1 - Rate transition with automatic Rate Transition block insertion:  On the Solver  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box for your model, the option Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer  is selected, and the parameter Deterministic data transfer  is set to Never  or Whenever possible . In your model, the software automatically inserts a Rate Transition block to transition a signal from a faster rate to slower rate (for periodic rate transition), or from a higher-priority rate to a lower-priority rate (for asynchronous rate transition). The data type of the input signal is atomic.  Scenario 2 - Rate transition with a virtual bus input:  In your model, a Rate Transition block has a virtual bus input. In the Rate Transition block parameters dialog box, the option Ensure data integrity during data transfer  is selected, and the option Ensure deterministic data transfer (maximum delay)  is cleared. The Rate Transition block transitions a signal in the virtual bus from a faster rate to a slower rate (for periodic rate transition), or from a higher-priority rate to a lower-priority rate (for asynchronous rate transition). The data type of the signal is atomic.
Workaround: To work around this bug in the automatic block insertion scenario, you must avoid using automatic block insertion when the other criteria of the scenario are met.To work around this bug in the virtual bus input scenario, you can replace the virtual bus input with individual signals, inserting a Rate Transition block for each individual signal that requires it.

Summary: Custom colors set on a Simulink object may be lost on save.
Description: If you apply a custom color to a Simulink model background, block or annotation foreground, or background that has both red and green components (set to 1), the color will appear correctly on screen, but will report to the command line and save into the model as "white." The custom setting will therefore be lost on save and load. For example: >> set_param(gcs, 'ScreenColor', '[1 1 0]') >> get_param(gcs, 'ScreenColor') ans = white
Workaround: Setting a color that is close to your desired color, but with either the red or green component not quite 1 will succeed.For example: >> set_param(gcs, 'ScreenColor', '[0.95 1 0]') >> get_param(gcs, 'ScreenColor') ans = [0.950000, 1.000000, 0.000000]

Summary: Segmentation fault when running Simulink Design Verifier
Description: If a model contains a Mux or Demux block, a segmentation fault might occur when running the Simulink Design Verifier.

Summary: Incorrect mask prompts and values for some masks containing Japanese characters
Description: When loading some masked blocks, the mask prompt strings and values may display incorrectly. The problem occurs only when the last character in a prompt or value is a Japanese character. Not all Japanese characters cause this problem. Instead of displaying one prompt string for each dialog entry, the mask displays two or more strings for the same entry, separated by pipe characters ("|"), and later entries are misaligned. Entries at the end of the mask dialog may display a placeholder string, e.g. "Mask Parameter 5". Similar problems can occur with the values in the mask dialog if they contain Japanese characters.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, make sure you include an ASCII character at the end of each prompt and value string. For example, you can add a space character at the end of each prompt or value.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect reuse of function-call initiator blocks
Description: Code generation might incorrectly reuse two or more function-call initiator blocks under all of the following conditions: The inputs or outports of the function-call subsystem must connect to the function-call initiator block The function-call subsystem blocks, called by the function-call initiator blocks, must be identical The function-call initiator block must also be identical The input or output connectivity of those function-call initiator and function-call subsystem pairs do not match  The function-call initiator can be a S-Function block, Subsystem block, MATLAB block, or Stateflow chart. The attached model minvalid_fcn_call_reuse.mdl  is an example of this modeling pattern. In this example, the function-call initiator block is a Stateflow chart.
Workaround: Download the attached zip file and extract the P-code MATLAB script findInvalidCodeReuse.p. To determine if the model might produce this failure, execute this p-script with the model name as an argument. For example,>> invalidReuse = findInvalidCodeReuse('minvalid_fcn_call_reuse')returns a structured cell array of size one, that contains two block handles indicating invalid code reuse. You can locate these subsystems using the hilite_system command. For example,>> hilite_system(invalidReuse{1}.blkHdls)highlights blocks that might be incorrectly reused in the generated code.To work around this problem, for each of the highlighted blocks, open the Subsystem Block Parameter dialog box. On the Code Generation pane, set the Function packaging parameter to either Inline or Function.

Summary: Model update may fail if a bus object with complex bus elements is specified for an Outport block
Description: You may receive an error similar to the following if all the conditions listed below exist: Complex signal mismatch. Input port 1 of 'modelname/Subsystem/Out1' expects a signal of numeric type real. However, it is driven by a signal of numeric type complex. At model update time, you may receive a message similar to this if all  of these conditions are true:  The Outport block specifies a bus object for its data type. The bus object has at least one complex bus element. The incoming bus is virtual.
Workaround: You can use either of these two workarounds: Remove the bus object specification from the Outport block dialog box.Use a non-virtual bus.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator can produce incorrect code or a segmentation violation for data defined as an alias type for Simulink.StructType
Description: The code generator can produce incorrect code (more or less padding than expected) or a segmentation viloation when using data defined as an alias type for Simulink.StructType . This bug also affects accelerated simulation targets. This bug does not  affect Simulink.Bus  types, because Simulink does not support alias types for Simulink.Bus .
Workaround: Define data to use a Simulink.StructType directly, instead of using an alias for Simulink.StructType.

Summary: On all Windows platforms (32bit & 64bit) using drag-n-drop to copy diagram elements between Simulink Editor windows may paste the diagram elements with an incorrect offset from the mouse location.
Description: When the horizontal or vertical scroll offsets are greater than zero on the target window for a drag-n-drop copy operation between Simulink subsystem windows, the copied elements' locations will be offset incorrectly. This offset is directly proportional to the scrollbar offset values. ? ?
Workaround: There are ways to work around this issue:Use Fit-to-view (spacebar) on the target window to remove scrollbar offsets before dropping, orUse the Simulink clipboard operations copy/paste to copy and paste between the subsystem windows??

Description: Double-clicking on a SimRF Equivalent Baseband block brings up a wrong dialog in many situations. Meanwhile, the following message shows up in the MATLAB command window. No appropriate method, property, or field Block for class Simulink.SLDialogSource.

Summary: Model simulation build failure or missing code coverage data when using Microsoft Visual Studio Version 9.0 Express, 10.0 Express, or 10.0 Professional
Description: When generating code for a model simulation using Microsoft Visual Studio Version 9.0 Express or 10.0 Express, build errors including the phrase 'not recognized ' might occur. For example: ### Building the Accelerator target for model: mymodel'"C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio 10.0\Common7\Tools\..\..\VC\bin\vcvars64"' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file.'nmake' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file.The make command returned an error of 9009'An_error_occurred_during_the_call_to_make' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file.### Real-Time Workshop build procedure for model: 'mymodel' aborted due to an error.  Also, when running a Software-in-the-loop (SIL) simulation with code coverage enabled using Visual Studio Version 9.0 Express, 10.0 Express, or 10.0 Professional, coverage data might be missing, which generates a warning that includes the following text: The model is configured to collect code coverage measurements. However, the coverage file has not been created.
Workaround: To work around these bugs, download the attached .zip file and extract its contents to your MATLAB installation tree, using the following steps:On your system, log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.Locate your MATLAB installation root folder by entering the matlabroot command in the MATLAB Command Window. This folder is referred to as matlabroot in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the .zip file attached to this Bug Report, attachment_673259_10b_2010-11-09.zip, and place the file in the matlabroot folder.Examine the contents of the .zip file and, for each file, make a backup copy of the equivalent file in your MATLAB installation tree.Extract the files from the .zip file into your MATLAB installation tree.

Summary: Code generation for a Constant block with a structure parameter and a constant sample time might not build
Description: Models with a Constant block that has all  of the following attributes might fail to build: Constant block specifies a structure parameter for its value. Constant block has a constant sample time.  The output signal from the Constant block has a test point.

Summary: (Lookup Table Performance) Interpolation Using Prelookup block causes simulation slowdown
Description: The Interpolation Using Prelookup block causes a simulation slowdown when the data type of the selection port ('sel') is double .
Workaround: To prevent the slowdown, set the data types of the index and selection ports to be uint32.

Summary: Adding a flow graph using the Pattern Wizard while editing a state label causes assertion failures
Description: When editing the label of a graphical function or subcharted state, if you select an option in the Patterns  menu before committing the label edits, you get assertion failure error messages in the command window. The assertion failures are harmless and do not cause any data or session loss.
Workaround: Close and reopen the chart window to return to a proper editable state.

Summary: Certain blocks with input signal of at least 2 dimensions might cause segmentation fault during code generation
Description: A model might fail to generate code if it contains one of the following blocks and the input signal has at least 2 dimensions: Complex to Magnitude-Angle Math Function MinMax MinMax Running Resettable Reciprocal Sqrt Sqrt
Workaround: Insert a Reshape block before the block to reshape the signal to one dimensional.

Summary: Switch block with variable-sized inputs asserts during code generation
Description: Real-Time Workshop code generation, or simulating a model using Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator mode, causes the following assertion when the model includes a Switch block with at least one variable-size input: Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: rtwcg_utils/rtwcg_dataconnector.cpp line 399: MatrixSubExpr::isa(dims_expr->outData())"

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier property-proving report is missing parameter values for counterexamples.
Description: The Simulink Design Verifier property-proving analysis report is missing parameter values for the counterexamples.

Summary: Cannot specify the output data type of the Discrete Derivative block as a fixed-point data type or an expression
Description: When a user specifies the output data type of a Discrete Derivative block as a fixed-point data type or an expression, this specification does not take effect. Instead, the compiled output data type is always set to int16 . This bug does not affect output data types specified as inheritance rules or built-in data types (for example, int8 ).
Workaround: You can work around this problem by modifying the model as follows:Insert a Signal Specification block immediately after the Discrete Derivative block.Set the output data type of the Signal Specification block to the type you wanted to specify on the Discrete Derivative block.Set the output data type of the Discrete Derivative block to Inherit: Inherit via back propagation.If modifying the model is not an option, you may choose to replace the simulink.mdl file. If you choose this option, follow these installation instructions:Installation InstructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems:Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Subsystem analysis fails when referencing Simulink.Signal objects resolved to Data Store Memory blocks inside the subsystem.
Description: If the blocks inside a subsystem reference Simulink.Signal  objects that are resolved by Data Store Memory blocks inside the subsystem, Simulink Design Verifier subsystem analysis generates errors. The sldvextract  function also generates an invalid model from these subsystems.

Summary: Japanese appears as squares in Simulink Model Discretizer for some Windows display settings
Description: On Japanese Windows XP machines, the Japanese appears as squares in the Simulink Model Discretizer when the Display Properties dialog box has the following settings on the Appearance tab: Windows and buttons  is set to Windows Classic style . Color scheme  is set to Windows Standard .
Workaround: To work around the display issue, complete these steps before launching MATLAB:Open the Display Properties dialog box and click the Appearance tab.Select a different setting for Windows and buttons, such as Windows XP style.Select a different setting for Color scheme, such as Default.If you complete those steps before launching MATLAB, Japanese should appear correctly in the Simulink Model Discretizer.

Summary: Generating code for a model referenced in normal mode that contains a Stateflow chart, Stateflow truth table, or Embedded MATLAB Function block may cause a segmentation violation
Description: Generating code for the top model that references a model causes a segmentation violation if the referenced  model meets all  of these conditions: It is referenced in Normal mode. It contains a Stateflow chart, Stateflow truth table, or Embedded MATLAB Function block. It uses External mode as the Data Exchange Interface for code generation.
Workaround: Reference the model in Accelerator mode instead of Normal mode.

Summary: On Windows 7, compiling MEX-files using OpenWatcom might generate the error "Compiler returned a bad status".
Description: On Windows 7, if the MATLAB installation path contains a space, for example: C:\Program Files\MATLAB  compiling a MEX-file using the OpenWatcom compiler generates the following error: Error! E1073: Invalid option '-I"C:\Program' Error! E1139: Command line contains more than one file to compile Unable to open 'Files\MATLAB\R2010b"\extern\include.c' Error: Compiler returned a bad status compiling "<filename>"

Summary: volatile qualifier not generated for constant block I/O of standalone subsystems
Description: In R2010b, Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder added support for generating constants with the storage type qualifier volatile . However, this functionality was not enabled for constant block I/O of standalone subsystems.

Summary: Simulink models with TimeScopes can cause MATLAB to become unresponsive shortly into a simulation
Description: Simulating a Simulink model that uses TimeScopes for several seconds can cause MATLAB to stop responding. This issue can also occur when a figure contains a uipanel and the uipanel FontUnits  property is set to 'normalized '. To reproduce the issue, execute the following code in MATLAB 7.11 (R2010b) on any platform. uipanel('Title','MyTitle','FontUnits','normalized')  At this point, MATLAB does not respond. You need to halt the application and restart.
Workaround: You can work around this issue by downloading the attached file, patch_g667610.jar:1. Download the attachment and save the file into folder matlabroot\java, where matlabroot is the result of typing matlabroot at the command prompt.2. In the MATLAB Command Window, type cd(fullfile(matlabroot,'java')); unzip patch_g667610.jar3. Restart MATLAB.When you simulate your model or use normalized FontUnits in uipanels, MATLAB should now remain responsive.If you deploy an application that this issue can affect, also extract the patch to mcrroot/java/.The mcrroot is the location of the MCR (MATLAB Compiler Runtime library) used to run the compiled application.cd(fullfile(mcrroot,'java')); unzip patch_g667610.jar?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Complex to Real-Imag block outputs incorrect imaginary value of variable-size signal
Description: If you configure a Complex to Real-Imag block to output the imaginary part of a variable-size signal of real values, the output of the block is not zero when simulating the model in Accelerator  mode or executing the generated code for the model. This only occurs for non-complex variable-size signals. For an example, see the attached model and MATLAB file.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, insert a Real-Imag to Complex block before the Complex to Real-Imag block in question. Configure the Real-Imag to Complex block to specify zero as a parameter for the imaginary part.?

Description: The Model Advisor MAAB check Check for mismatches between names of Stateflow ports and associated signals (db_0123)  and IEC 61508 check Check usage of Stateflow constructs  issue an incorrect warning when an input to a Stateflow chart is a bus signal.

This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file, corresponding to your release, associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code for an unconditionally zero initialized Merge block
Description: Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder eliminates a necessary zero initialization of an output variable of a Merge block in the generated root level model initialize function when all of the following conditions are true: Remove internal data zero initializaiton  is selected on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box In the Merge block parameter dialog, the Initial output  is specified as 0  (zero). The Merge block is not inside a conditionally executed subsystem The Merge block is connected to a Concat block  The attached model mmissing_merge_init.mdl  illustrates the modeling pattern that exposes this problem.
Workaround: The zero initialization elimination function in Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder is multi-staged. Download the attached zip archive and extract the P-code MATLAB script. To disable the part of this optimization causing the incorrect zero initialization elimination, call the MATLAB script disableExtendedZeroInitElimination with a 1 or true as an argument. The return value of disableExtendedZeroInitElimination is the previous setting. Example:>> disableExtendedZeroInitElimination(1)ans = 0Alternatively, you can disable this optimization by clearing Remove internal data zero initialization on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.?

Summary: Passing Data Store Memory data by address in Stateflow chart crashes MATLAB
Description: In a Stateflow chart, passing Data Store Memory data to a custom C code function by address crashes MATLAB.
Workaround: Define a chart-parented local data with the same type, size, and complexity as the Data Store Memory data.Pass the address of this local data to the custom C function after assigning its value from Data Store Memory data.For example:my_local_data = my_dsm_data;myfcn(&my_local_data);my_dsm_data = my_local_data; % if "myfcn" modifies the input data.

Summary: Code generation error when the build folder path name contains single quotes
Description: The following error occurs when generating code in a build folder for which the path name contains single quotes: Internal error while creating code interface description file: codeInfo.mat. Aborting code generation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Calling reusable system which accepts bus, vector, or matrix input
Description: Code generation of a child reusable subsystem might generate incorrect code when its input is a bus defined with an ImportedExternPtr  storage class. In this case, the parent might apply the address-of operator when passing the bus argument to the child subsystem. For example, child(&busptr, ...) when the generated code should be child(busptr, ...) The C compiler produces a warning or error about the signature and call site mismatch. ?
Workaround: Use ImportedExtern storage class for a bus signal that is input to a reusable subsystem.

Summary: Requirements report fails to correctly include links to DocBlock contents
Description: Creating links from Simulink model objects to the contents of RTF DocBlock blocks in the same or another Simulink model is a new feature in R2010a. Links are highlighted and navigated correctly, but creating a requirements report for a model that includes such links produces the following warnings: Warning: Unresolved relative path: ModelName:[some number].rtf In C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2010b\toolbox\slvnv\reqmgt\+rmiut\full_path.p>full_path at 44  In C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2010b\toolbox\slvnv\reqmgt\+rmisl\locateFile.p>locateFile at 18  In C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2010b\toolbox\slvnv\reqmgt\@RptgenRMI\doc2path.p>doc2path at 8  In C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2010b\toolbox\slvnv\reqmgt\@RptgenRMI\@ReqDocTable\execute.p>execute at 33  The Document Table at the top of the report shows the following errors : ERROR: unable to locate ModelName:[same number].rtf  Clicking any such link in a Document Table in the requirements report results in the "File or URL Not Found " message from the browser, or current working folder window may open depending on the browser. Similar links in Requirmements Tables in the body of the Report will also not work. ?
Workaround: Installation instructions (Windows only)Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder. Use R2010a or R2010b patch depending on your installed MATLAB version.Make a backup copy of each of the *.m files listed in the .zip file and/or of each corresponding *.p file. Remove the originals.Extract the files from the .zip file to your <MATLAB> folder.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.
