Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong answer returned when using a vector of fixed-point or Simulink.IntEnumType initial values
Description: In Stateflow, the initial value for data objects can be set in the Model Explorer using a scalar or vector value. When setting the initial value of a data object using either a vector of type fixed-point or Simulink.IntEnumType, the value is not correctly preserved. For example, if a user sets the initial value of a local data object to be: [fi(1, 1, 16, 0) fi(2, 1, 16, 0)] then the actual value being used will be: [1 1]  instead of [1 2]  In other words, the value of the first element will be duplicated for all the elements of the vector or matrix.
Workaround: The workaround is to cast the initial value vectors of type fixed-point or Simulink.IntEnumType to double.For instance, in the case of a Simulink.IntEnumType class called Colors with 'Red' and 'Blue' enumerations, replace the following:[Colors.Red Colors.Blue]withdouble([Colors.Red Colors.Blue])

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Optimization combines two consecutive shift operations into one
Description: The code generator incorrectly optimizes the following expression: (x << 16U) << 16U The optimization converts it to: x << 32U In C, the semantics of shifting a 32-bit integer by 32 bits or more is undefined. This optimization might cause the generated code to compute incorrect answers. To determine whether your generated code is affected by this bug, use the following command to find all shifts by a constant: grep -E "(>>|<<)(=)?[[:space:]]+[0-9]+" *.c If none of the shift distances exceeds the bit size of the operand, then your code is not affected by the bug.

Summary: MATLAB crash when Weighted Sample Time Math block uses 'Inherit via internal rule' in model with continuous sample time
Description: When the following conditions are all true, MATLAB might crash without any helpful output: The sample time of a Simulink model is continuous. The model has a Weighted Sample Time Math block where the output data type is set to 'Inherit via internal rule'.

Summary: If a referenced model contains a block with one zero-crossing signal and this referenced model is in an enabled subsystem, code generation will cause compilation failure
Description: If a referenced model contains a block with one zero-crossing signal, and this referenced model resides in an enabled subsystem, code generation will cause a compilation failure with following errors: Level2.c(125) : error C2143: syntax error : missing ')' before ';' Level2.c(125) : error C2102: '&' requires l-value.
Workaround: In the file matlab/rtw/c/tlc/lib/syslib.tlc, you can change the following line:real_T *zcsv = (real_T *) &(%%%);toreal_T *zcsv = (real_T *) &(%%); .

Summary: Model Advisor check Check configuration parameters for MISRA-C:2004 compliance recommendation might increase MISRA-C:2004 violations
Description: The Model Advisor check Check configuration parameters for MISRA-C:2004 compliance  recommends clearing the parameter Shift right on a signed integer as arithmetic shift  on the Hardware Implementation  > Embedded hardware  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. Depending on your model and your embedded hardware implementation settings, following the recommendation might increase the number of MISRA-C:2004 violations. Clearing this parameter might reduce violations of rule 12.7, but might cause additional violations of MISRA-C:2004 rules 10.1 and 10.3.
Workaround: After running the Model Advisor check Check configuration parameters for MISRA-C:2004 compliance, you can choose to not clear the configuration parameter Shift right on a signed integer as arithmetic shift. In the Action box, do not click Modify All to automatically modify the configuration parameters.

Summary: Simulink block data tips options can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: If you enable block data tips options (from the Simulink View  menu or from the MATLAB command line) for a model that does linearlization or PID controller tuning, then hovering over a subsystem crashes MATLAB after model compilation.
Workaround: When you use linearization or PID controller tuning in a model, disable each of the block data tips options, by using the Simulink model window View -> Block Data Tips Options menu options or command line parameters (shown in parentheses):Block Name (BlockNameDataTip)Parameter Names And Values (BlockParameterDataTip)User Description String (BlockDescriptionStringDataTip)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation: size of variable sized signal is not updated correctly by Embedded MATLAB function block
Description: This bug can occur when an Embedded MATLAB function block updates the size of a variable sized signal. It can only occur when the x=foo(x) idiom is used inside the Embedded MATLAB function block. For example: function y1 = libs_scalar_in5(u,y) %#eml  y1 = myfcn(u,y);function y = myfcn(u,y)  eml.inline('never');  if u == 1    y = zeros(3);  end The generated code for this example does not change the size of the output to 3x3 when u==1.
Workaround: Do not use the x=foo(x) idiom on variable sized signals.

Summary: Incorrect analysis results for models that contain Discrete-Time Integrator blocks with external initial conditions connected to Constant blocks.
Description: The Constant block connected to the Discrete-Time Integrator block's external initial condition input is not initialized. This causes incorrect analysis results.
